notes: Fixed EM features preserved from checkpoint 02, rewritten in the generated-feature function format to guide LLM code generation.

features.description: Example fixed feature: Jaccard overlap of normalized row value sets.
Example fixed feature: similarity of non-empty cell counts.
Example fixed feature: token Jaccard on serialized row text.
Example fixed feature: normalized edit similarity on serialized row text.
Example fixed feature: overlap of row-level numeric value sets.
Example fixed feature: max directional containment between normalized row value sets.
features.code:
def compute_feature(ctx):
    stats_a = ctx["stats_a"]
    stats_b = ctx["stats_b"]
    set_a = stats_a["value_set"]
    set_b = stats_b["value_set"]
    inter_size = len(set_a.intersection(set_b))
    contain_a = float(inter_size) / float(len(set_a)) if len(set_a) > 0 else 0.0
    contain_b = float(inter_size) / float(len(set_b)) if len(set_b) > 0 else 0.0
    if contain_b > contain_a:
        return contain_b
    return contain_a

features.description: Example fixed feature: IDF-weighted Jaccard on row token sets, emphasizing rarer matching tokens.
Example fixed feature: Jaccard overlap on header-aware value tokens, exposing whether similar content appears under similar fields.
Example fixed feature: similarity from relative median numeric difference.
features.code:
def compute_feature(ctx):
    stats_a = ctx["stats_a"]
    stats_b = ctx["stats_b"]
    med_a = float(stats_a["numeric_median"])
    med_b = float(stats_b["numeric_median"])
    denom = max(abs(med_a), abs(med_b), 1.0)
    rel = abs(med_a - med_b) / float(denom)
    return 1.0 / (1.0 + rel)
